*�����������������4A moving head light such as this (Chauvet Maveric Force S Spot in 29 channel mode) has various dmx channels of which some are 16 bit while others are 8 bit. To simplify this process, you can specify a DMX channel's value resolution on the DMX Fixture POP itself by setting the Value Resolution parameter appropriatly. 
FOr this particular fixture, there are some values that are great to be used normalized (like pan / tilt - Position) while for others, like Strobe or control for the wheels, it is more practical to use the DMX 0-255 value range.

The fixture itself can be described as a single point with all attributes specified there. With the single point you can now also multiply (copy it), do your attribute lookups after and send all the points that represent the same fixture into a single DMX Fixture POP.

The DMX Map DAT shows how it's using all points to create channel mappings for all the fixtures. For demonstration purposes we are adding a channel gap between all fixtures, which will insert n DMX channels inbetween each fixture. Also enabled is "Quantize Universe" making sure that if a fixture does not fit into a universe it will start in the next subsequent one.

The fixture here has a single light source. You can find the DMX Chart here: https://www.chauvetprofessional.com/products/maverick-force-s-spot/

*�����������������PWhen dealing with multiple fixtures of the same type, it is possible to specify the dmx address by primitive attribute.

The attribute names are:
* DMXFixtureNet (uint)
* DMXFixtureSubnet (uint)
* DMXFixtureUniverse (uint)
* DMXFixtureChannel (uint)
* DMXFixtureNetAddress (uint4)

In this example they are specified with the copy template and the created as primitive attributes by setting the Destination parameter of the Copy POP to Primitive.

The fixture here has a single light source. You can find the DMX Chart here: https://www.chauvetprofessional.com/products/maverick-force-s-spot/

*�����������������In this example of the Chauvet Maverick Storm 3 BeamWash, the LEDs of the outer ring have Dimmer, Strobe and RGB control while the main LEDs have Dimmer, Strob, and RGBA control. So the 2 sets of geometry must be treated seperatly ontop of being seperate from the Pan-Tilt platform they are on. For this requirement we can use the Group parameter of each DMX Channel sequence block. This example has a total of 424 channels. The main rule is still that each fixture is represented by a single primitive. While here initially 2 Linestrips are created with their respective attributes, after merging, the primitives are deleted and recreated as a single primitve.
 
The attribue combine is used to copy the body channels onto the single resulting primitive. As a result the whole fixture can again be copied with a Copy POP and via lookups induvidually controlled while still all accessible via the single DMX Fixture POP.

You can find the DMX Chart here: https://www.chauvetprofessional.com/products/maverick-storm-3-beamwash/
